description_long: This sand patch is an irregularly shaped mound of fine, light tan sand with a gently undulating surface. The patch features a central raised area with subtle ridges and shallow depressions, giving it a natural, wind-sculpted appearance. The edges of the sand patch are uneven and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, lacking any sharp boundaries. The surface texture is granular and slightly rough, with visible micro-contours and small ripples typical of loose sand. There are no visible foreign objects, debris, or signs of recent disturbance on the surface. The overall form suggests a naturally accumulated sand deposit, possibly found in an open, urban-adjacent area such as a playground, construction site, or roadside.
description: An irregular, light tan sand patch with a gently mounded, rippled surface and gradual, uneven edges. The texture is granular and natural, with no visible debris or disturbance.
description_view_0: From this angle, the central mound and surrounding shallow depressions are most prominent, with the uneven, sloping edges clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the elongated shape of the sand patch and the gentle rise of the central area, with subtle surface ridges extending outward.
description_view_2: The central mound appears more pronounced from this perspective, and the gradual blending of the sand into the surrounding area is evident.
description_view_3: This side shows the sand patch's flatter edge and the gentle slope leading up to the central mound, with visible surface undulations.
attribute_location: playground, construction site, or roadside in an urban area
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding surface, with no sharp or well-defined edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: The sand patch appears partially blended with its surroundings, with a natural transition at the edges.
